channel.title: Health on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Health on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Prescription drugs are the 3rd cause of all US deaths, 4th cause of deaths in hospital
item.description:
Prescription Drugs Are the Leading Cause of Death

Brownstone

  • “37,309 drug deaths were reported to the FDA in 2006 and
    123,927 ten years later, which is 3.3 times as many”

  • “Depression drugs kill many people, mainly among the elderly, because they can cause orthostatic hypotension, sedation, confusion, and dizziness. The drugs double the risk of falls and hip fractures in a dose-dependent manner,8,9 and within one year after a hip fracture, about one-fifth of the patients will have died. As elderly people often fall anyway, it is not possible to know if such deaths are drug deaths.”

item.title: Early-Onset diseases and Vitamin D - several studies
item.description:
Candidate causes include:
Low vitamin D (which can be fought by Vitamin D)
((Microplastics now causing problems in most life forms - many studies Microplastics))
((Ultra-processed foods associated with worse health and lower Vitamin D - several studies Ultra-processed foods))
((Air Pollution reduces Vitamin D Air pollution))
((DDT and other pesticides decrease vitamin D Pesticides))
      • Why are so many young people getting cancer? - April 2024
      • 40,700 studies had EARLY-ONSET in their title as of April 2024

Why are so many young people getting cancer? - April 2024

VOX

item.title: 5% of the world consumes 55% of the prescription medications while lifespan is decreasing (US)
item.description:
• Quote from FLCCC email - March 2024
      • VitaminDWiki - Longevity and healthspan increased by Vitamin D - many studies
      • VitaminDWiki - US RDA Vitamin D math mistakes cost the world about one Trillion dollars annually
      • VitaminDWiki - Prescription drugs are the 3rd cause of all US deaths, 4th cause of deaths in hospital - April 2024

Quote from FLCCC email - March 2024

Download the email PDF from VitaminDWiki

item.title: US IRS now favors drugs over wellness
item.description:
• IRS Cracks Down on Deductions for Healthy Food, Exercise as Childhood Chronic Disease Epidemic Explodes
      • VitaminDWiki - Why Big Pharma is ‘TERRIFIED’ of Vitamin D - Dr. Korry March 2024
      • VitaminDWiki - 5% of the world consumes 55% of the prescription medications while lifespan is decreasing (US) – March 2024

IRS Cracks Down on Deductions for Healthy Food, Exercise as Childhood Chronic Disease Epidemic Explodes

ChildrenHealthDefense

Includes a link to the 40 minute podcast

item.title: Ultra-processed foods associated with worse health and lower Vitamin D - several studies
item.description:
• Ultra-processed food and adverse health outcomes – BMJ meta-analysis Jan 2024
      • The Perils of Ultra-processed foods (article and book) - July 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - Ultra-processed food consumption: 2X increased risk of vitamin D deficiency – Dec 2023
      • Ultra-processed foods increase the risk of metabolic syndromes - Jan 2024
      • Increased Ultra-processed foods increase risks of Cancer and CVD by 9% - Lancet Dec 2023
      • Ultra-processed foods can be as addictive as cocaine or smoking - Feb 2024
      • Perplexity AI on ultra-processed foods - March 2024

Ultra-processed food and adverse health outcomes – BMJ meta-analysis Jan 2024

Credible increased risk of 9 health problems (a subset of table)

item.title: Which health problems take more than 10 years to develop - Chat-GPT
item.description:
• Chat-GPT answer
      • Note: All of the slow developing health problems are related to low vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki - Maternal vitamin D deficiency can result in problems 1 or 2 decades later - May 2023
      • This is one of the articles in the AI category (most of which are summaries or defintions)

Chat-GPT answer

Several health conditions can take more than 10 years to develop, often due to a combination of genetic, lifestyle, and environmental factors. Here’s a list of some notable conditions that typically have a long development period:

item.title: 16 Longevity books compared in a video
item.description:
• Peter Attia’s Longevity Book Outlive: The BEST or WORST longevity book?
      • VitaminDWiki - Longevity and healthspan increased by Vitamin D - many studies
      • VitaminDWiki - How Not To Age book (diet, exercise, supplements) Dr. Greger - Dec 2023

Peter Attia’s Longevity Book Outlive: The BEST or WORST longevity book?

YouTube 35 minutes

Books

  1. Outlive: The Science and Art of Longevity by Peter Attia

item.title: Chronic Health Conditions: 27% of Americans have more than 1 of the top 10 - CDC 2018
item.description:
Prevalence of Multiple Chronic Conditions Among US Adults, 2018

CDC Sept 2020

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Top 10 chronic conditions(before COVID and Long-COVID)

  1. arthritis,

  2. cancer,

  3. chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD),

  4. coronary heart disease,

  5. current asthma,

  6. diabetes,

  7. hepatitis,

  8. hypertension,

  9. stroke, and

  10. weak or failing kidney

#00F:All of the above are prevented/treated by Vitamin D

VitaminDWiki - Many Chronic Health Diseases and Infections can controlled by 40 ng of Vitamin D – Aug 2023


Markdown:
--------

#### Prevalence of Multiple Chronic Conditions Among US Adults, 2018

 **[CDC Sept 2020](https://www.cdc.gov/pcd/issues/2020/20_0130.htm?ck_subscriber_id=19612225)** 

 **[<i class="fas fa-file-pdf" style="margin-right: 0.3em;"></i>Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki ](/attachments/d3.mock.pdf)** 

##### Top 10 chronic conditions(before COVID and Long-COVID)

1. arthritis, 

1. cancer, 

1. chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), 

1. coronary heart disease, 

1. current asthma, 

1. diabetes, 

1. hepatitis, 

1. hypertension, 

1. stroke, and 

1. weak or failing kidney

 **<a href="/posts/health-problems-and-d" style="color: red; text-decoration: underline;" title="This post/category does not exist yet: Health Problems and D">~~#00F:All of the above are prevented/treated by Vitamin D~~</a>** 

---

#### VitaminDWiki - [Many Chronic Health Diseases and Infections can controlled by 40 ng of Vitamin D – Aug 2023](/posts/many-chronic-health-diseases-and-infections-can-controlled-by-40-ng-of-vitamin-d)

<!-- ~tc~ (alias(Chronic Health Condition:  27% of Americans have more than 1 - CDC 2018)) ~/tc~ -->

<!-- ~tc~ (alias(Chronic Health Conditions:  27% of Americans have more than 1 of the top 10 - CDC 2018)) ~/tc~ -->

AST Structure:
-------------
├── HeadingNode
│   full_match: `!!!!Prevalence of Multiple Chronic Conditions Among US Adults, 2018`
│   inner_content: `Prevalence of Multiple Chronic Conditions Among US Adults, 2018`
│   level: `4`
│   children:
│   ├── TextNode
│   │   full_match: `Prevalence of Multiple Chronic Conditions Among US Adults, 2018`
│   │   inner_content: `Prevalence of Multiple Chronic Conditions Among US Adults, 2018`├── TextNode
│   full_match: `\n`
│   inner_content: `\n`├── BoldNode
│   full_match: `__[https://www.cdc.gov/pcd/issues/2020/20_0130.htm?ck_subscriber_id=19612225|CDC Sept 2020]__`
│   inner_content: `[https://www.cdc.gov/pcd/issues/2020/20_0130.htm?ck_subscriber_id=19612225|CDC Sept 2020]`
│   children:
│   ├── LinkNode
│   │   full_match: `[https://www.cdc.gov/pcd/issues/2020/20_0130.htm?ck_subscriber_id=19612225|CDC Sept 2020]`
│   │   inner_content: `CDC Sept 2020`
│   │   url: `https://www.cdc.gov/pcd/issues/2020/20_0130.htm?ck_subscriber_id=19612225`
│   │   children:
│   │   ├── TextNode

│   │   │   inner_content: `CDC Sept 2020`├── TextNode
│   full_match: `\n`

│   full_match: `{FONT(size="20")}__{ATTACH(inline="1" id="20424" icon="1")}Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki {ATTACH}__{FONT}`
│   inner_content: `__{ATTACH(inline="1" id="20424" icon="1")}Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki {ATTACH}__`
│   attrs_dict:
│   │   raw_content: size="20"
│   │   size: 20
│   children:
│   ├── BoldNode
│   │   full_match: `__{ATTACH(inline="1" id="20424" icon="1")}Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki {ATTACH}__`

{FONT(size="20")}__{ATTACH(inline="1" id="20424" icon="1")}Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki {ATTACH}__{FONT}

#arthritis, 
#cancer, 
#chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), 
#coronary heart disease, 
#current asthma, 
#diabetes, 
#hepatitis, 
#hypertension, 
#stroke, and 
#weak or failing kidney
__((Health Problems and D|~~#00F:All of the above are prevented/treated by Vitamin D~~))__
---
!!!!VitaminDWiki - ((Many Chronic Health Diseases and Infections can controlled by 40 ng of Vitamin D – Aug 2023))
~tc~ (alias(Chronic Health Condition:  27% of Americans have more than 1 - CDC 2018)) ~/tc~
~tc~ (alias(Chronic Health Conditions:  27% of Americans have more than 1 of the top 10 - CDC 2018)) ~/tc~

item.title: Stem cells and Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
Markdown:
--------


AST Structure:
-------------
├── HeadingNode
│   full_match: `!!!!~~#F06:13+~~  VitaminDWiki pages have STEM CELL in the title`
│   inner_content: `~~#F06:13+~~  VitaminDWiki pages have STEM CELL in the title`
│   level: `4`
│   children:
│   ├── ColorNode
│   │   full_match: `~~#F06:13+~~`
│   │   inner_content: `13+`
│   │   attrs_dict:
│   │   │   raw_content: #F06
│   │   children:
│   │   ├── TextNode
│   │   │   full_match: `13+`
│   │   │   inner_content: `13+`
│   ├── TextNode
│   │   full_match: `  VitaminDWiki pages have STEM CELL in the title`
│   │   inner_content: `  VitaminDWiki pages have STEM CELL in the title`├── TextNode
│   full_match: `\n`
│   inner_content: `\n`├── EmphasisNode
│   full_match: `''This list is automatically updated''`
│   inner_content: `This list is automatically updated`
│   children:
│   ├── TextNode
│   │   full_match: `This list is automatically updated`
│   │   inner_content: `This list is automatically updated`├── TextNode
│   full_match: `\n`
│   inner_content: `\n`├── ListNode
│   full_match: `{LIST()}\n{list max="50"}\n{filter field="title" content="\"STEM CELL \""}\n{filter type="wiki page"}\n{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}\n{OUTPUT(template="table")}\n{tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}n\n{column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}\n{column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}\n{OUTPUT}\n{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}\n{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}\n{LIST}`
│   inner_content: `\n{list max="50"}\n{filter field="title" content="\"STEM CELL \""}\n{filter type="wiki page"}\n{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}\n{OUTPUT(template="table")}\n{tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}n\n{column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}\n{column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}\n{OUTPUT}\n{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}\n{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}\n`
│   attrs_dict:
│   │   raw_content: 
│   children:
│   ├── TextNode
│   │   full_match: `\n{list max="50"}\n{filter field="title" content="\"STEM CELL \""}\n{filter type="wiki page"}\n{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}\n{OUTPUT(template="table")}\n{tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}n\n{column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}\n{column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}\n{OUTPUT}\n{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}\n{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}\n`
│   │   inner_content: `\n{list max="50"}\n{filter field="title" content="\"STEM CELL \""}\n{filter type="wiki page"}\n{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}\n{OUTPUT(template="table")}\n{tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}n\n{column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}\n{column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}\n{OUTPUT}\n{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}\n{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}\n`├── TextNode
│   full_match: `\n`
│   inner_content: `\n`

Original Tiki:
-------------
!!!!~~#F06:13+~~  VitaminDWiki pages have STEM CELL in the title
''This list is automatically updated''
{LIST()}
{list max="50"}
{filter field="title" content="\"STEM CELL \""}
{filter type="wiki page"}
{sort mode="modification_date_major_desc"}
{OUTPUT(template="table")}
   {tableparams allowtableexpansion="n" shownbitems="y" title=""}n
    {column sort="title" label="Title" field="title" mode="raw"}{column}
    {column label="Modified" sort="modification_date_major" field="modification_date_major"}
{OUTPUT}
{FORMAT(name="title")}{display name=title format="objectlink"}{FORMAT}
{FORMAT(name="modification_date_major")}{display name=modification_date_major format="date"}{FORMAT}
{LIST}

item.title: Down syndrome people have even lower D levels if have additional Vitamin D diseases
item.description:
• Hypovitaminosis D in persons with Down syndrome and autism spectrum disorder
      • VitaminDWiki - Down syndrome and low vitamin D - several studies

Hypovitaminosis D in persons with Down syndrome and autism spectrum disorder

J Neurodev Disord . 2023 Oct 25;15(1):35. doi: 10.1186/s11689-023-09503-y.

Natalie K Boyd 1, Julia Nguyen 2, Mellad M Khoshnood 1, Timothy Jiang 1, Lina Nguyen 1, Lorena Mendez 1, Noemi A Spinazzi 3, Melanie A Manning 4, Michael S Rafii 5 6, Jonathan D Santoro 7 8

item.title: Doctors are incentivised to prescribe medicines, not improve health
item.description:
• How PCPs are penalized for positive outcomes from lifestyle change

How PCPs are penalized for positive outcomes from lifestyle change

MD Edge

One example: Dr. reduced diabetes by lifestyle changes

  • However, the CMS 5-Star Rating System assigned the primary care physician a grade of C rather than A, which put the physician›s 5-star rating at risk. Why? Because the system bases its score largely on medication compliance . The physician was penalized despite achieving the optimal health outcome, and at a lower cost than with medication. "

Medicare incentivizes disease management, not disease reversal

item.title: Live 24 years longer if have 8 habits (survey of 700,000 veterans)
item.description:
• The eight habits researchers recommend to lengthen life expectancy
      • What habits will extend life expectancy?
      • See also: Longevity and healthspan increased by Vitamin D - many studies 80+ studies
      • See also: Ways to improve health

The eight habits researchers recommend to lengthen life expectancy

URL

item.title: US Healthcare spending to consume 20% of GDP by 2028
item.description:
• Modern HealthCare March 2022
      • It appears that Vitamin D can cut the cost of healthcare in half

Modern HealthCare March 2022

It appears that Vitamin D can cut the cost of healthcare in half

Hope to have a demonstration with 5,000 people in 2024 getting 50,0000 IU weekly


Markdown:
--------
{{< toc >}}

---

#### [Modern HealthCare](https://www.modernhealthcare.com/healthcare-economics/healthcare-spending-consume-20-gdp-2028#:~:text=As%20a%20result%2C%20the%20healthcare,which%20was%2017.7%25%20of%20GDP.) March 2022

---

#### It appears that Vitamin D can cut the cost of healthcare in half

 **Hope to have a demonstration with 5,000 people in 2024 getting 50,0000 IU weekly**

!!!!It appears that Vitamin D can cut the cost of healthcare in half
__Hope to have a demonstration with 5,000 people in 2024 getting 50,0000 IU weekly__

item.title: Rare diseases: 1 in 10 Americans (some are helped by Vitamin D) - 2023
item.description:
There are more than 10,000 known rare diseases.

Approximately 80% of rare diseases are estimated to have a genetic origin.

Of those, 70% start in childhood. (56% of rare diseases start in childhood)

  • “In the United States, about half of the people with a rare disease are children. Many of these diseases are chronic and worsen over time.”

The US defines incidence of a “rare disease” as being 1 in every 1,500 people
